item.title: The Meaning of Word Knowledge or Gyan or Jnyana in Bhagwat Gita
item.author: hari@dasarpai.com (Dr. Hari Thapliyaal)
item.description:
The Meaning of Word Knowledge in Bhagwat Gita
#

In the Sanskrit language, there is a word “ज्ञान” (romanized as Gyan or Jnyana), which translates to “Knowledge” in English. However, in a worldly sense, when we talk about knowledge, we usually refer to information about something—generally objects, people, processes, or events around us. Sometimes, it also refers to our own body; for a slightly deeper thinker, it includes the mind and ego as well. But for a spiritual seeker, this “something” is not an external thing or “thing” at all but the Self.

item.title: The Essence of Bhagwat Gita: 4th Chapter
item.author: hari@dasarpai.com (Dr. Hari Thapliyaal)
item.description:
The Essence of Bhagwat Gita: 4th Chapter
#

The fourth chapter of Bhagwat Gita is Jnyana (Knowledge) Yoga. It has 42 slokas. What is the meaning of Jnyana or Knowledge in the context of Bhagwat Gita and Hindu Scriptures? What is the power of Knowledge? What is the role of this in Liberating person from suffering? These are the ideas dicussed here.
